items: What are you thankful for and what has been challenging for you since we last met?
Have you experienced God at work in your life lately?
Have you had the opportunity to care for your friends or neighbours this week?
How did you go with your application from the last time we met?
Read the passage aloud and then watch the video clip.
Have one person retell the passage in their own words with the group’s help.
Was there anything missed or added?

data.html:
3The teachers of the law and the Pharisees brought in a woman caught in adultery. They made her stand before the group4and said to Jesus, “Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of adultery. 5 In the Law Moses commanded us to stone such women. Now what do you say?”6They were using this question as a trap, in order to have a basis for accusing him.

But Jesus bent down and started to write on the ground with his finger. 7 When they kept on questioning him, he straightened up and said to them, “Let any one of you who is without sin be the first to throw a stone at her.”8Again he stooped down and wrote on the ground.

9At this, those who heard began to go away one at a time, the older ones first, until only Jesus was left, with the woman still standing there. 10 Jesus straightened up and asked her, “Woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?”

11“No one, sir,” she said.

“Then neither do I condemn you,” Jesus declared. “Go now and leave your life of sin.”

items: What stands out for you in this passage?
What does this passage tell you about Jesus, God, people and you?
What does this passage say about judgment?
If this passage is from God, what difference would it make in your life?
How are you going to apply this passage this week?
Who will you share this passage with?
